creator: lihong@ieee.org
text:
On Tue, Jan 27, 2009 at 7:16 AM, Steve Ames <steve@energistic.com> wrote:
> On Mon, Oct 27, 2008 at 11:01:13AM -0400, Steve Ames wrote:
>> On Mon, Oct 27, 2008 at 08:36:12AM +0800, Eric L. Chen wrote:
>> > New patch for devel/ptlib
>>
>> Great. I, also, think this is the right way to go.
>
> Eric, have you given thought to submitting a port to add devel/ptlib
> so that net/opal and such can use it instead of the, outdated, pwlib?
>
> I don't have any interest in maintaining that new port (I will if you
> don't want to). However if you would like to submit a PR to add that
> port it'd be great. I'll help get PRs submitted to move applications
> from pwlib to ptlib.
>
> If you don't have anything to start with you might want to take a look
> at http://steve.energistic.com/h323-ports/ptlib.tgz. Its a FreeBSD port
> for ptlib 2.2.4 based on your patch from PR-128140. If it looks good
> and you want to be port Maintainer then by all means submit a PR to get
> it added. If you don't then I'll submit the PR.
>
> In all cases I consder PR-128140 to be closeable since upgrading
> devel/pwlib per that PR causes way more problems than it solves.
>
> Let me know.
>
> -Steve
>
OK, I will submit new PR to add ptlib and take maintenance..

/Eric
